And it was changed to:

I need to determine if changing these conditions caused any adjacent faults. Adjacent faults usually refer to issues that arise from changes in nearby code lines, especially those that might interact with each other.

In the mutant change, only line 370 was altered. The surrounding lines (like 369 and 371) are either part of the same if-else block or directly related to calculating offsets and handling overflows. 

Since the change affects how overflow is detected, it's possible that other parts of the code might now behave differently when dealing with time zone calculations. However, from the provided data, only one test (TestMonthDay_Properties) transitioned from failing to passing, while others remained in their original state.

I don't see any immediate signs that this change caused adjacent faults because it seems localized to the overflow condition check. The other tests either weren't affected or continued to fail for reasons unrelated to this specific code change.
